title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Trump Vows America Will Answer After Iran Shoots Down U.S. Helicopter
stories.summary:
President Donald Trump announced on Tuesday that the United States will respond after Iran shot down a U.S. Apache helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz. The incident occurred during a patrol operation, prompting immediate military and political reactions from Washington.

According to reports from multiple outlets, including Al Jazeera and the BBC, two crew members aboard the aircraft were successfully rescued by an American sea drone following the crash. President Trump confirmed that both pilots are uninjured and safe, emphasizing their well-being in his initial statements.

Despite the successful recovery of the personnel, Trump stated that Washington must respond to the attack as a matter of necessity. The White House has not yet detailed the specific nature of the planned retaliation, but officials have signaled that a response is imminent given the severity of the incident.

links.headline: Trump says Iran shot down US helicopter over Hormuz, vows to respond
links.excerpt: US president says although the pilots were rescued, Washington 'must, of necessity, respond to this attack'.
links.imageAlt: Trump says Iran shot down US helicopter over Hormuz, vows to respond

stories.title: ICC Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan Suspended After Sexual Misconduct Investigation
stories.summary:
The International Criminal Court has suspended Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan following a two-year investigation into allegations of sexual misconduct. The suspension was announced late Monday after the probe examined long-standing claims against him.

Khan denies all accusations and his legal team strongly rejects the court's decision to suspend him. The BBC reported that Khan maintains his innocence regarding the harassment allegations.

links.headline: ICC suspends top prosecutor after investigating misconduct allegations
links.excerpt: Karim Khan denies all allegations of sexual misconduct and his lawyers say he rejects the decision in the strongest terms.

stories.title: During Xi visit, North Korea's Kim voices support for China's claim to Taiwan
stories.summary:
Chinese President Xi Jinping concluded a two-day state visit to North Korea on Tuesday, marking his first official trip to Pyongyang since 2019. The visit was characterized by elaborate ceremonial displays, though official statements regarding the enhancement of bilateral relations remained notably vague.

During the summit, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un explicitly voiced support for China’s position regarding Taiwan. This endorsement aligns with Beijing's longstanding claim over the self-governing island, signaling a diplomatic reinforcement between the two nations amidst broader regional geopolitical tensions.

links.headline: Xi Jinping and Kim Jong Un vow stronger ties as North Korea visit wraps up
links.excerpt: Xi has wrapped up a two-day visit to Pyongyang, his first official trip to North Korea since 2019.

stories.title: Man reportedly shot at Kenya protest against US Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary:
Kenyan police have cracked down on demonstrators protesting the establishment of a US-run Ebola quarantine centre near Nanyuki. The confrontation occurred as protesters marched to voice their opposition to the facility, which has sparked significant local unrest.

Concerns among the public center on potential cross-border infection risks and a perceived lack of transparency from the government regarding the treatment centre's operations. Reports indicate that tensions escalated during the demonstration, leading to clashes between marchers and law enforcement officers.

stories.title: Judge finds suspect accused of killing Iryna Zarutska incompetent to stand trial for now
stories.summary:
A federal judge has ruled that Decarlos Brown Jr. is incompetent to stand trial for the alleged murder of Ukrainian refugee Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte, North Carolina light rail train last year.

U.S. Attorney Russ Ferguson confirmed the determination on Tuesday, stating that Brown will be hospitalized for treatment to address his mental health condition before any legal proceedings can resume.

links.headline: Charlotte train stabbing suspect dodges potential death penalty for now after he's ruled incompetent
links.excerpt: Decarlos Brown Jr., accused of fatally stabbing Ukrainian woman Iryna Zarutska on a Charlotte light-rail train, is deemed incompetent to stand trial.
links.imageAlt: Charlotte train stabbing suspect dodges potential death penalty for now after he's ruled incompetent

stories.title: WATCH: Fights Break Out Across NYC After Knicks Lose Game 3
stories.summary:
Violence erupted across New York City on Monday night following the New York Knicks' home loss to the San Antonio Spurs in Game 3 of the NBA Finals. Although authorities had banned watch parties outside Madison Square Garden due to security concerns surrounding President Donald Trump's attendance, unregulated gatherings at other locations throughout the city turned into all-out brawls.

In a separate political development, California Republican Steve Hilton has advanced in the state’s gubernatorial race. According to reports, Hilton will now compete against former Biden administration Secretary of Health and Human Services Xavier Becerra for the governorship.

links.headline: Republican Steve Hilton Advances in California Gubernatorial Race
links.excerpt: California Republican gubernatorial candidate Steve Hilton advanced in the state's gubernatorial race and will compete against former Biden administration Secretary of Health and Human Services Xavier Becerra. stories.title: Disgraced Crypto Fraudster Sam Bankman-Fried Formally Seeks Trump Pardon
stories.summary:
Sam Bankman-Fried has formally submitted a request for a presidential pardon to Donald Trump, aiming to clear the fraud convictions associated with his collapsed cryptocurrency enterprise. The former FTX executive, often described as a disgraced figure in the digital asset industry, is seeking official clemency to erase the legal penalties resulting from his business failures.

Multiple outlets, including Breitbart and the Washington Times, have reported on the development, highlighting the high-profile nature of the request. Bankman-Fried’s appeal marks a significant step in his post-conviction efforts to regain freedom and restore his reputation following widespread scrutiny of his financial practices.

links.headline: Disgraced Crypto Fraudster Sam Bankman-Fried Formally Seeks Trump Pardon
links.excerpt: Disgraced crypto tycoon Sam Bankman-Fried has filed a formal request for a pardon from President Donald Trump to erase the fraud charges tied to his failed cryptocurrency business. Spectator Australia has published an opinion piece defending Barnaby Joyce following his recent interview on Sky News Australia with Andrew Bolt. The author notes that while the broadcast initially caused dismay, they ultimately concluded that Joyce acted appropriately during the exchange.

The article suggests that public reaction to the interview was mixed, with some observers criticizing the content. However, the publication argues that Joyce’s stance was justified given the context of the discussion and his political position.

stories.title: Mickey Mouse and friends freeze Super Netball finals out of prime venue
stories.summary:
The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new benchmark for attendance, drawing a record crowd to one of the country's largest stadiums. This significant milestone highlighted the sport's growing popularity and commercial appeal during its most recent championship event.

However, the momentum appears to have stalled for the 2026 season, as organizers have been forced to move the finals series to smaller venues. The primary cause for this logistical retreat is a scheduling conflict with Disney on Ice, which has secured the prime stadium dates previously available to netball.

links.headline: Mickey Mouse and friends freeze Super Netball finals out of prime venue
links.excerpt: The 2025 Super Netball Grand Final set a new standard with a record crowd at one of the biggest stadiums in the country. But in 2026, the sport has been forced to retreat to smaller finals venues due to a scheduling issue with Disney on Ice.

stories.title: BHP files: leaked memo shows miner backtracking on key climate projects in Australia – video
stories.summary:
Documents leaked to the Guardian and ABC’s Four Corners reveal that BHP has delayed or halted major climate initiatives at its Western Australian iron ore operations. The mining giant reportedly war-gamed strategies to stall significant investments in these projects through the next two decades.

In response, BHP stated that achieving net zero emissions relies on technological advancements for trucks, trains, and dozers that are not yet commercially viable. The company maintains that progress is contingent upon the deployment of these specific innovations.

links.headline: BHP files: leaked memo shows miner backtracking on key climate projects in Australia – video
links.excerpt: The world’s biggest miner has halted or delayed projects to cut vast amounts of emissions and has quietly war-gamed options to stall major climate investments in its Western Australian iron ore operations into the next two decades, according to documents leaked to the Guardian and ABC’s Four Corners. In a statement, BHP said its progress towards net zero emissions was dependent on technological shifts in trucks, trains and dozers, which were not yet ready to be deployed. <a href
links.imageAlt: BHP files: leaked memo shows miner backtracking on key climate projects in Australia – video

stories.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
stories.summary:
Anthropic has released Claude Fable 5, marking the first time its Mythos-class model architecture is available to the public. This release represents a significant step in making advanced AI capabilities accessible outside of restricted research environments.

The model includes specific safety guardrails designed to block responses in high-risk domains such as cybersecurity and biology. These restrictions aim to mitigate potential dangers associated with powerful generative models while still allowing broader public use.

stories.title: Fitbit’s Charge 6 and Ace LTE are now as cheap as the new $100 Air
stories.summary:
Apple is introducing new generative AI photo editing tools in iOS 27, unveiled at WWDC 2026. The company is positioning these features as a way to enhance user creativity through advanced image manipulation capabilities.

The announcement highlights Apple's growing focus on integrating artificial intelligence into its core software ecosystem. These updates aim to provide users with more sophisticated options for modifying and enhancing their photos directly within the operating system.

stories.title: Doctors and NHS could be sued for mistakes made by AI tools, report warns
stories.summary:
A report from the Medical Protection Society warns that doctors and the NHS could face medical negligence lawsuits for errors committed by artificial intelligence tools used in patient diagnosis and treatment planning. The organization is urging ministers to overhaul current liability laws, which currently hold healthcare professionals accountable even when AI systems are responsible for harmful outcomes.

Under existing legal frameworks, medics can be held liable if patients are harmed or die due to mistakes made by AI technology. This creates significant risk for healthcare providers who increasingly rely on these tools to assist in clinical decision-making processes.

links.headline: Doctors and NHS could be sued for mistakes made by AI tools, report warns
links.excerpt: Medical Protection Society calls for law to be overhauled to help medics avoid liability for errors made by technology Doctors and the NHS could be sued for medical negligence over mistakes made by artificial intelligence tools used in diagnosing patients and suggesting their treatment, ministers are being warned. Under the law as it stands, medics and the health service can be held liable for patients being harmed or dying even if it was AI that made the errors that resulted in t

stories.title: Bank of England warns of AI scams as deepfakes of Farage-Bailey fight spread
stories.summary:
The Bank of England has issued a warning to the public regarding AI-generated scams following the viral spread of deepfake videos featuring Governor Andrew Bailey and Nigel Farage. The fabricated clips, which depict the two men physically clashing in a Question Time studio setting, have circulated widely on social media platform X.

Governor Bailey highlighted that AI-generated content targeting central banks is increasingly prevalent online. He urged citizens to remain vigilant against such deceptive material and encouraged them to report any suspicious videos they encounter.

links.headline: Bank of England warns of AI scams as deepfakes of Farage-Bailey fight spread
links.excerpt: Governor urges people to report videos on X that falsely show the men clashing in the Question Time studio The Bank of England has warned the public against falling for AI-generated scams after deepfake videos of Nigel Farage fighting its governor spread online. Andrew Bailey, the head of the BoE, said AI-generated content related to central banks was spreading and urged people to be “vigilant”. <a href="https://www.theguardian.com/business/2026/jun/09/bank-of-england-ai-scam
links.imageAlt: Bank of England warns of AI scams as deepfakes of Farage-Bailey fight spread

stories.title: Plan for AI legal assistants in England and Wales ‘cannot replace funding and staff’, lawyers say
stories.summary:
Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy is set to announce a trial of AI-powered legal assistants in the crown courts of England and Wales. The initiative aims to reduce the significant backlog of court cases by integrating virtual assistants into judicial proceedings.

Legal professionals have responded with caution, warning that the technology must not be used as a substitute for essential funding and additional court staff. Critics emphasize that while AI may offer efficiency gains, it cannot replace the vital human resources required to maintain the integrity and functionality of the legal system.

links.headline: Plan for AI legal assistants in England and Wales ‘cannot replace funding and staff’, lawyers say

stories.title: NEA mobilizes task force to restore power in quake-hit Mindanao
stories.summary:
The National Electrification Administration (NEA) has reactivated Task Force Kapatid to restore electricity in Southern Mindanao following a magnitude 7.8 earthquake that struck Sarangani and surrounding provinces.

Administrator Antonio Mariano Almeda convened an emergency meeting with member electric cooperatives to coordinate the response for approximately 803,230 affected consumer connections.

As part of this mobilization, the Cotabato Electric Cooperative, Inc. deployed six linemen to General Santos City to assist South Cotabato Electric Cooperative II in repairing damaged infrastructure.

links.headline: Cotelco sends linemen to aid power restoration in quake-hit GenSan
links.excerpt: COTABATO CITY – Six linemen from the Cotabato Electric Cooperative, Inc. (Cotelco) have been deployed to General Santos City as part of the “Task Force Kapatid” program to help restore electricity in areas served by South Cotabato Electric Cooperative (Socoteco II) that were affected by the magnitude 7.8 earthquake. stories.title: Angara directs rapid disaster response for quake-hit Mindanao schools
stories.summary:
Education Secretary Sonny Angara has ordered a swift disaster response for 267 schools in Mindanao that were severely damaged by the recent 7.8 magnitude earthquake.

The Department of Education is deploying emergency classrooms, learning packets, and clean-up crews to the affected regions to restore educational services as quickly as possible.

Secretary Angara stressed that while resource deployment is being fast-tracked, the immediate safety of students and staff remains the top priority in all relief operations.

links.headline: Angara directs rapid disaster response for quake-hit Mindanao schools

stories.title: Palace: No constitutional crisis, Gatchalian is sole Senate leader
stories.summary:
The Malacañang Palace has dismissed concerns of a constitutional crisis regarding the leadership of the Senate, affirming that Sherwin Gatchalian is the sole recognized leader of the chamber. This stance was taken despite repeated assertions by Senator Alan Peter Cayetano that he remains the legitimate Senate president.

Palace press officer Claire Castro stated that the executive branch only acknowledges Gatchalian as the Senate President Pro Tempore and sees no constitutional crisis in this position. The clarification comes amid ongoing political tensions between the two senators over who holds the rightful leadership of the upper house.

In a related development, Senator Cayetano has questioned the credibility of intelligence regarding a potential security threat within the Senate. He criticized the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) for providing information he described as coming from a "clearly partisan" source.

This skepticism follows earlier statements by Gatchalian, who cited NBI reports about a possible security risk in the chamber. The conflicting views highlight the deepening rift between the two political figures and their respective camps.

links.headline: Cayetano questions credibility of Senate threat intel from NBI
links.excerpt: MANILA, Philippines — Sen. Alan Peter Cayetano questioned on Tuesday the credibility of a reported Senate security threat, saying the information came from a “clearly partisan” National Bureau of Investigation (NBI). Earlier, Senate President Pro Tempore Sherwin Gatchalian said there was a potential security threat in the chamber, citing the NBI’s information. Cayetano said not
links.imageAlt: Cayetano questions credibility of Senate threat intel from NBI

stories.title: Peace adviser lauds BARMM solidarity with quake-affected communities
stories.summary:
Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation and Unity Mel Senen Sarmiento praised the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) for its humanitarian response to a recent natural disaster. The region extended assistance to communities in neighboring Soccsksargen and Davao regions that were impacted by a magnitude 7.8 earthquake.

Sarmiento highlighted the deployment of the BARMM Rapid Damage Assessment team as part of this solidarity effort. The gesture underscores regional cooperation in addressing the aftermath of the seismic event affecting multiple areas in Mindanao.

links.headline: Peace adviser lauds BARMM solidarity with quake-affected communities

stories.title: DOST to unveil Mt. Pinatubo marker to recall 1991 eruption
stories.summary:
The Department of Science and Technology – Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (DOST-PHIVOLCS) will commemorate the 35th anniversary of the Mount Pinatubo eruption with a series of events starting Tuesday. The commemoration aims to recall the historic 1991 disaster that significantly impacted Pampanga and global climate patterns.

In partnership with the Holy Angel University Center for Kapampangan Studies, DOST-PHIVOLCS will host a twin-event program titled “Pinatubo at.” A key component of these activities is the unveiling of a new marker dedicated to remembering the eruption. The initiative seeks to preserve the memory of the event while highlighting scientific monitoring efforts.

links.headline: DOST to unveil Mt. Pinatubo marker to recall 1991 eruption

stories.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
stories.summary:
Vanke, one of China's most prominent property developers, has reported a significant financial loss, signaling that the nation's real estate crisis is deepening. The company's struggles have intensified concerns about the broader health of the sector and whether state intervention will be necessary to stabilize it.

The report highlights the growing vulnerability of even well-established firms within China's property market. Investors and analysts are now closely watching for signs of further distress among other major developers, as the crisis continues to spread across the industry.

links.headline: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
links.excerpt: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.
links.imageAlt: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes

stories.title: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
stories.summary:
The tech industry is poised to replace the familiar FAANG acronym with a new designation: MANGOS. This shift reflects the emergence of a fresh class of corporate leaders as major private companies prepare for significant public market entries.

SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI are all reportedly eyeing massive public debuts in the near future. Their anticipated listings suggest a changing landscape where these high-profile firms may soon join or surpass traditional tech giants in influence and valuation.

links.headline: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.
links.excerpt: With SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI all eyeing massive public debuts, the tech industry may soon have a new class of corporate overlords — and a new acronym to match. Say goodbye to FAANG and hello to MANGOS.
links.imageAlt: It’s not FAANG anymore. It’s MANGOS.

stories.title: Ghost in the Machine review – entertaining AI polemic dives into its dark history in race politics and eugenics
stories.summary:
Valerie Veatch’s documentary Ghost in the Machine examines the historical roots of artificial intelligence within right-wing ideology and eugenics. The film explores this controversial lineage through a series of colorful, often eccentric figures who shaped early AI concepts.

Veatch, known for previous documentaries such as Love Child and Me at the Zoo, brings her observational style to this polemic on technology's darker origins. The review describes the work as an entertaining dive into how race politics influenced the development of machine intelligence.

links.headline: Ghost in the Machine review – entertaining AI polemic dives into its dark history in race politics and eugenics
links.excerpt: The roots of AI in rightwing ideology is examined in Valerie Veatch’s enjoyable doc, including an array of colourful, often crazed, figures Director Valerie Veatch made her name with documentaries such as Love Child (about an online gaming-addicted couple whose child died of malnutrition) and Me at the Zoo (about American vlogger Cara Cunningham), film

stories.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
stories.summary:
Israeli medical officials have revealed that freed hostages from the October 7 attacks still had shrapnel embedded in their bodies. The findings highlight the severe physical trauma endured during captivity, with some individuals requiring ongoing medical attention for injuries sustained during the initial assault and subsequent detention.

Reports indicate that several female hostages were held in isolation for extended periods. One official noted that some women spent up to eight months confined within tunnels, enduring harsh conditions that contributed to their long-term health complications.

links.headline: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
links.excerpt: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.
links.imageAlt: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack

stories.title: The Guardian view on NHS records: patients are not raw material for big tech | Editorial
stories.summary:
The Guardian editorial urges ministers to terminate Palantir’s contract with the NHS, warning that patient confidentiality is at risk. The publication argues that patients should not be treated as raw material for big tech companies driven by an appetite for public data.

Alarm bells reportedly sounded last month when it emerged that Palantir engineers could gain unlimited access to identifiable NHS patient records. Such sensitive medical information was intended to be available only under strict conditions, raising concerns about the scope of corporate access.

links.headline: The Guardian view on NHS records: patients are not raw material for big tech
links.excerpt: Ministers should end Palantir’s contract before medical confidentiality is sacrificed to Silicon Valley’s appetite for public data Alarm bells ought to have rung when it emerged last month that Palantir engineers could gain “ unlimited access ” to identifiable NHS patient data. Such sensitive medical information was only supposed to be available eithe
